summary: An Adriatic EU and NATO member that fought a war of independence in the 1990s, joined the eurozone and Schengen in 2023, and now thrives on tourism and shipping.

history.detail: Croatia enters a centuries-long dynastic union with Hungary.
Becomes part of the new South Slav kingdom after WWI.
War with Serb forces and the Yugoslav army begins.
Croatia retakes Serb-held territory, effectively ending the war.
Becomes the 28th member of the European Union.

history.event: Adopts the euro and joins Schengen
history.detail: Deep integration into Europe's core institutions.

key_doctrines: Support for Western Balkans EU enlargement

finance.central_bank: Croatian National Bank (within the ECB system)
leverage_tools: LNG terminal at Krk for EU energy security

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Small but punches above its weight; produced unicorns

resources.oil_gas: Modest domestic output; Krk LNG terminal diversifies supply
resources.water: Abundant; long Adriatic coastline and rivers
resources.agriculture: Wine, olives, maize; strong food and tourism link

follow_the_money.detail: The Adriatic coast drives ~20% of GDP, peaking each summer
Floating terminal supplies LNG to Croatia and central Europe
Cohesion and recovery funds finance major infrastructure

theories_disputed.detail: Croatia hails it as liberation; Serbia calls it ethnic cleansing of Krajina Serbs. ICTY acquitted top generals.

connections.detail: Key trade partner and source of tourists and investment
Lingering war legacy and border and minority disputes
Neighbour and fellow EU member; Piran Bay border dispute
